contributor.username: ja>壁掛成蛸は三考する葦である

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''闘鶏 大山主'''（つげ の おおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。 =…」
revision.text:
'''闘鶏 大山主'''（つげ の おおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。 == 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて[[闘鶏大山主|闘鶏稲置大山主]]（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす。
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。
== 皇后への暴言 == それから何年も立って、允恭天皇が即位し、（推定[[413年]]）忍坂大中姫が皇后となり、名代部として、「刑部」（おしさかべ）が制定された。皇后がまだ未婚だった時代、一人で苑で遊んでいた折に、
{{quotation|時に闘鶏国造（つげのくにのみやつこ）、傍（ほとり）の径（みち）より行（あり）く。馬に乗りて籬（まがき）に莅（のぞ）みて、皇后に謂（かた）りて、嘲りて曰はく、 「能くソノを作るや、汝（なびと）」といふ。且（また）曰はく、「圧乞（いで）、戸母（とじ）、其の蘭（あららぎ）一茎（ひともと）」といふ。
（そのとき闘鶏国造（つげのくにのみやつこ）が側の小道を通り、馬に乗って垣根越しに語りかけて、嘲っていうのには「うまく庭を作れるのかね、あんた」と言った。また言ったことには、「さあ、刀自（とじ）、そこの野蒜（のびる）を一本」と言った。}}
この闘鶏国造は、大山主のことと想定されている。このあと、数々のぞんざいな応対があり、皇后は、
{{quotation|「首（おびと）や、余（あれ）、忘れじ」}}（お前、私は忘れまいよ）
とおっしゃった。皇后になった忍坂大中姫は、馬にのって「蘭」（あららぎ）を求めたものを搜しあて、昔日の罪を責めて殺そうと思ったが、
{{quotation|「臣（やっこ）が罪、実に死（しぬる）に当れり。然れども其の日に当りては、貴（かしこ）き者（ひと）にましまさむといふことを知りたてまつらず」
（約：私の罪はまことに死罪に当ります。けれども、そのときには、貴いお方になられようとは知りませんでした）}}
と弁解したので、皇后は死刑を赦して、[[カバネ|姓]]をおとして[[稲置]]にした<ref>『日本書紀』允恭天皇2年2月14日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(二)、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == *[[額田大中彦皇子]] *[[仁徳天皇]] *[[氷室]] *[[允恭天皇]] *[[忍坂大中姫]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけ の おおやまぬし} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

'''闘鶏 大山主'''（つげ の おおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。 == 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて闘鶏稲置大山主（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす」
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。

（そのとき闘鶏国造（つげのくにのみやつこ）が側の小道を通り、馬に乗って垣根越しに語りかけて、嘲っていうのには「うまく庭を作れるのかね、あんた」と言った。また言ったことには、「さあ、刀自（とじ）、そこの野蒜（のびる）を一本」と言った。}}

{{quotation|「首（おびと）や、余（あれ）、忘れじ」（お前、私は忘れまいよ）}}

と弁解したので、皇后は死刑を赦して、[[カバネ|姓]]をおとして[[稲置]]にした<ref>『日本書紀』允恭天皇2年2月14日条</ref>。

以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。

== 皇后への暴言 == それから何年も立って、[[允恭天皇]]2年（推定[[413年]]）、[[忍坂大中姫]]（おしさか の おおなかつひめ）が皇后となり、[[名代]]部として、「[[刑部]]」（おしさかべ）が制定された。皇后がまだ未婚だった時代、一人で苑で遊んでいた折に、
{{quotation|時に[[闘鶏国造]]（つげのくにのみやつこ）、傍（ほとり）の径（みち）より行（あり）く。馬に乗りて籬（まがき）に莅（のぞ）みて、皇后に謂（かた）りて、嘲りて曰はく、 「能くソノを作るや、汝（なびと）」といふ。且（また）曰はく、「圧乞（いで）、戸母（とじ）、其の蘭（あららぎ）一茎（ひともと）」といふ。
（そのとき闘鶏国造（つげのくにのみやつこ）が側の小道を通り、馬に乗って垣根越しに語りかけて、嘲っていうのには「うまく庭を作れるのかね、あんた」と言った。また言ったことには、「さあ、刀自（とじ）、そこの野蒜（のびる）を一本」と言った。}}
この闘鶏国造は、大山主のことと想定されている。このあと、数々のぞんざいな応対があり、皇后は、
{{quotation|「首（おびと）や、余（あれ）、忘れじ」（お前、私は忘れまいよ）}}
とおっしゃった。皇后になった忍坂大中姫は、馬にのって「蘭」（あららぎ）を求めたものを捜し当て、昔日の罪を責めて殺そうと思ったが、
{{quotation|「臣（やっこ）が罪、実に死（しぬる）に当れり。然れども其の日に当りては、貴（かしこ）き者（ひと）にましまさむといふことを知りたてまつらず」
（約：私の罪はまことに死罪に当ります。けれども、そのときには、貴いお方になられようとは知りませんでした）}}
と弁解したので、皇后は死刑を赦して、[[カバネ|姓]]をおとして[[稲置]]にした<ref>『日本書紀』允恭天皇2年2月14日条</ref>。
== 祀る神社 == *[[氷室神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]春日野）

revision.comment: 修正
revision.text:
'''闘鶏 大山主'''（つげ の おおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]。'''闘鶏稲置大山主命'''や'''国造大山主君'''とも。
== 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて闘鶏稲置大山主（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす」
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。
== 祀る神社 == *[[氷室神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]春日野）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(二)、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == *[[額田大中彦皇子]] *[[仁徳天皇]] *[[氷室]] *[[天然氷]] *[[允恭天皇]] *[[忍坂大中姫]] *[[刑部]]

revision.text:
'''闘鶏大山主'''（つげのおおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[闘鶏国造]]'''の一人。'''闘鶏稲置大山主命'''や'''国造大山主君'''とも。
== 概要 == [[神八井耳命]]に始まる[[多氏]]族の[[国造]]の一つである[[闘鶏国造]]の人物で、『[[日本書紀]]』には[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]に氷を献じたと伝わる。
国造・'''[[赤目君]]'''の子で、兄弟に蛙手君がいる<ref>『異本阿蘇氏系図』</ref>。[[多氏]]、[[仲国造]]、[[長狭国造]]、[[印波国造]]、[[科野国造]]らと同族。
== 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて闘鶏稲置大山主（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす」
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。

'''闘鶏大山主'''（つげのおおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[闘鶏国造]]'''の一人。'''闘鶏稲置大山主命'''や'''国造大山主君'''とも。
== 概要 == [[神八井耳命]]に始まる[[多氏]]族の[[国造]]の一つである[[闘鶏国造]]の人物で、『[[日本書紀]]』には[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]に氷を献じたと伝わる。
国造・'''[[赤目君]]'''の子で、兄弟に蛙手君が、子に'''[[角古君]]'''がいる<ref>『異本阿蘇氏系図』</ref>。[[多氏]]、[[仲国造]]、[[長狭国造]]、[[印波国造]]、[[科野国造]]らと同族。
== 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて闘鶏稲置大山主（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす」
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。
== 祀る神社 == *[[氷室神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]春日野）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(二)、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == *[[額田大中彦皇子]] *[[仁徳天皇]] *[[氷室]] *[[天然氷]] *[[允恭天皇]] *[[忍坂大中姫]] *[[刑部]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけ の おおやまぬし}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:多氏]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|local chieftain|ja|豪族}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君). == Overview == Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni..."
revision.text:
'''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|local chieftain|ja|豪族}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} went hunting in {{ill|Tsuge|ja|闘鶏}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrines|ja|氷室神社}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷}} * {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} * {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}} * {{ill|Gyōbu|ja|刑部}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Tsuge no Oyamanushi]] to [[Tsuge no Ōyamanushi]]
revision.text:
'''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|local chieftain|ja|豪族}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} went hunting in {{ill|Tsuge|ja|闘鶏}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrines|ja|氷室神社}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷}} * {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} * {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}} * {{ill|Gyōbu|ja|刑部}}

revision.text:
'''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|local chieftain|ja|豪族}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} went hunting in {{ill|Tsuge|ja|闘鶏}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrines|ja|氷室神社}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Himuro|ja|氷室}} * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷}} * {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|ja|允恭天皇}} * {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|ja|忍坂大中姫}} * {{ill|Gyōbu|ja|刑部}}
[[Category:Figures in Japanese Mythology]] [[Category:Kofun Period Figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Figures]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Warriors and Officials]] [[Category:Year of Birth Unknown]] [[Category:Ta Clan]] [[ja:闘鶏大山主]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge no Ōyamanushi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge no Ōyamanushi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge no Ōyamanushi}}{{citation needed|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi}}

{{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}} happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} {{ill|local chieftain|ja|豪族}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).

Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|ja|長屋王}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|ja|氷室神社}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988
== Related Articles ==

revision.text:
'''闘鶏大山主'''（つげのおおやまぬし、生没年不詳）とは、『[[日本書紀]]』に登場する[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[闘鶏国造]]'''の一人。'''闘鶏稲置大山主命'''や'''国造大山主君'''とも。
== 概要 == [[国造]]の一つである[[闘鶏国造]]の人物で、『[[日本書紀]]』には[[仁徳天皇|仁徳朝]]に氷を献じたと伝わる。
== 都祁の氷室 == 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第十一によると、仁徳天皇62年（推定[[375年]]）、[[額田大中彦皇子]]（ぬかた の おおなかつひこ の みこ）は闘鶏（つげ）に狩猟に出かけた折、野原の中に廬（いお）のようなものがあるのを発見して、人を遣わして報告させると、窟（むろ）だということだった。そこで、土地の支配者である闘鶏大山主を呼び出して、それが何であり、どのような目的で使用するものかを尋ねた（闘鶏」（つげ）は[[大和国]][[山辺郡]]の地名で、[[奈良県]]旧[[都祁村]]、現[[奈良市]]に当たる）。
「因（よ）りて闘鶏稲置大山主（つけ の いなき おおやまぬし）を喚（め）して、問ひて曰（のたま）はく、
（額田：）『其（か）の野の中に有るは、何の窨（むろ）ぞ』（あの野の中にあるのは何のあなだ）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）啓（まう）して曰（まう）さく、『氷室なり』とまうす。
皇子の曰（のたま）はく、『其の蔵（をさ）めたるさま如何（いか）に。亦（また）奚（なに）か用（つか）ふ』（収蔵したさまはいかなるものなのか、また何に用いるのか）とのたまふ。
（大山主：）曰（まう）さく、『土を掘ること丈（ひとつゑ）余。草（かや）を以て其の上に蓋（ふ）く。敦（あつ）く茅荻（ちすすき）を敷きて、氷を取りて其の上に置く。（土を一丈(約3メートル)あまり掘り、草をその上に蓋としてかぶせて、厚くちがやや荻を敷いて、氷を取りその上に置きます。）
既に夏月（なつ）を経（ふ）るに泮（き）えず。其の用（つか）ふこと、即（すなは）ち熱き月に当りて、水酒に漬（ひた）して用（つか）ふ』（既に夏を越していますが、消えません。暑い月に水酒にひたして使います）とまうす」
以上のようなやりとりがあり、氷室にどのように氷を貯蔵すればよいのか理解した額田大中彦皇子は、其の氷を天皇に献上した。以後、朝廷では季冬（師走）には氷を貯蔵し、春分（如月）にくばるようになった、という。<ref>『日本書紀』仁徳天皇62年是歳条</ref>。
[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。
== 祀る神社 == *[[氷室神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]春日野）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(二)、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年
== 関連項目 == *[[額田大中彦皇子]] *[[仁徳天皇]] *[[氷室]] *[[天然氷]] *[[允恭天皇]] *[[忍坂大中姫]] *[[刑部]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけ の おおやまぬし}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:多氏]]

[[奈良市]]・[[京都市]]など全国の氷室神社では大山主は、額田大中彦皇子・仁徳天皇と並んで、祭神となっている。また、[[長屋王]]が氷室を持ち、牛乳を飲んでいたのは有名だが、都祁氷室を使用していたことが木簡から判明している。
== 祀る神社 == *[[氷室神社]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]春日野）

== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(二)、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（上）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年

{{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).

Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].

According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"

Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:People of ancient Japan
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==

Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Characters in Japanese mythology
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==

According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ancient Japanese Warriors and Officials
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>

Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==

[[sn:Tsuge no Ōyamanushi]] [[ja:闘鶏大山主]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Ō clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.comment: ±[[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]]→[[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]]<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories-->[[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==

== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

revision.comment: Bot: remove incorrect Shona (sn:) interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|qq=|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|qq=|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|qq=|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.
