== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Ice|1=Natural Ice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127553}} * [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] * [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] * [[:en:Ministry of Justice (imperial China)|Gyōbu]]

[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Ō clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]]<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories-->[[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q55177952}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
{{wikidata link|Q55177952}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mō Ujitani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Ice|1=Natural Ice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127553}} * [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] * [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] * [[:en:Ministry of Justice (imperial China)|Gyōbu]]
{{translated page|ja|闘鶏大山主|version=93871969|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>

Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "pages with conflicting qids" to "qq"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] [[:en:Gōzoku|local chieftain]] and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in [[:en:Cockfighting|Tsuge]] (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrines]] across Japan, including those in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]] and [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that [[:en:Prince Nagaya|Prince Nagaya]] owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* [[:en:Himuro Shrine|Himuro Shrine]] (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)

* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * [[:en:Ice house (building)|Himuro]] * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Ice|1=Natural Ice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127553}} * [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]] * [[:en:Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime]] * [[:en:Ministry of Justice (imperial China)|Gyōbu]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55177952]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=The cockfighting confusion seems to come from the fact that {{nihongo|cockfighting|闘鶏|tsuge}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local chieftain}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tsuge}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|WD=Q253893|lt=Himuro}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrines}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|en|Prince Nagaya|WD=Q1963492|lt=Prince Nagaya}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrine}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mō Ujitani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988

}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local chieftain}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tsuge}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|WD=Q253893|lt=Himuro}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=chigaya grass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrines}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|en|Prince Nagaya|WD=Q1963492|lt=Prince Nagaya}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrine}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* *Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 2), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1994 * *Complete Modern Translation of Nihon Shoki* (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mō Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mō Ujitani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988

* {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} * {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|WD=Q253893|lt=Himuro|1=Ice house (building)}} * {{ill|Natural Ice|ja|天然氷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Ice|1=Q22127553|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127553}} * {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} * {{ill|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|en|Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|WD=Q11072207|lt=Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime|1=Oshisaka no Ōnakatsuhime}} * {{ill|Ministry of Justice (imperial China)|en|Ministry of Justice (imperial China)|WD=Q711784|lt=Gyōbu|1=Ministry of Justice (imperial China)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:闘鶏大山主]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
happened within Ice Houses (Himuro). A bit confusing as to why they did them there of all places but gotta work on finding more sources to make all of this clear. Increasing support for conflating {{ill|Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|ja|闘鶏大山主|1=Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Ōyamanushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Himuro Gongen|ja|氷室権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氷室}} but still no hard proof[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:58, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsuge no Ōyamanushi''' (闘鶏大山主, birth and death years unknown) was a {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|WD=Q84066|lt=local chieftain|1=Gōzoku}} and one of the rulers of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. He is also referred to as '''Tsuge Inaki Ōyamanushi no Mikoto''' (闘鶏稲置大山主命) and '''Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōyamanushi no Kimi''' (国造大山主君).
== Overview ==
Tsuge no Ōyamanushi was a figure of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, one of the ancient Japanese administrative units. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he presented ice to the imperial court during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.
== The Himuro of Tsuge ==
According to volume 11 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 62nd year of Emperor Nintoku's reign (estimated to be 375 CE), {{ill|Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55177820}} went hunting in {{ill|Cockfighting|en|Cockfighting|WD=Q319771|lt=Tsuge|1=Cockfighting}} (present-day {{ill|Tsuge, Nara City|ja|奈良市都祁村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge, Nara City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}). There, he discovered something resembling a hut in the middle of a field. When he sent someone to investigate, it was identified as a cave (muro). He summoned Tsuge no Ōyamanushi, the local ruler, to inquire about its purpose.
{{quote| Prince Nukata: "What is that cavity in the middle of the field?"
Ōyamanushi: "It is a {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|WD=Q253893|lt=Himuro|1=Ice house (building)}} (ice chamber)."
Prince Nukata: "How is it stored, and what is it used for?"
Ōyamanushi: "We dig the ground to a depth of over one jō (approximately 3 meters), cover it with grass, and place thick layers of {{ill|chigaya grass|ja|茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigaya grass|1=Q11617144|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617144}} and reeds. The ice is then placed on top. Even after the summer passes, it does not melt. It is used during the hot months by soaking it in water and sake." }}
After this exchange, Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko understood how to store ice and presented it to the emperor. From that time on, the imperial court began storing ice during the month of {{ill|Shiwasu|ja|師走|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 12月|1=December|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126}} (December) and distributing it during {{ill|Kisaragi|ja|如月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisaragi|1=Kisaragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21073620}} (February).<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Nintoku, 62nd Year of Reign.</ref>
At {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrines|1=Himuro Shrine}} across Japan, including those in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, Ōyamanushi is enshrined alongside Prince Nukata no Ōnakatsuhiko and Emperor Nintoku. Additionally, it is well-known that {{ill|Prince Nagaya|en|Prince Nagaya|WD=Q1963492|lt=Prince Nagaya|1=Prince Nagaya}} owned a Himuro and consumed milk, with wooden tablets confirming the use of the Tsuge Himuro.
== Shrines Dedicated to Himuro ==
* {{ill|Himuro Shrine|en|Himuro Shrine|WD=Q11549570|lt=Himuro Shrine|1=Himuro Shrine}} (Kasugano, Nara City, Nara Prefecture)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
